key_insight: The paper shows that natural-language FEM workflows can be made reliable by constraining the LLM to front-end parsing and geometry tasks while keeping solver templates fully deterministic.
engineering_relevance: This design lowers setup effort for multi-physics finite element simulations in FEniCS without letting the model generate the critical solver core, improving trustworthiness for engineering use.
method: LLM parses user prompts into structured JSON
LLM generates Gmsh code only for non-catalog geometries with retry/validation
A deterministic dispatcher maps the validated spec to five human-written FEniCS/UFL templates
evidence_signal: On a 15-prompt parser benchmark, final valid parse rate reached 100.0% with 97.1% field-extraction accuracy, and the real LLM-to-Gmsh path achieved 90.0% success on 10 custom-geometry cases.
watch_item: The approach still depends on front-end parsing and geometry validity, so malformed or ambiguous prompts can fail before the deterministic solver stage.
paper_title: A Constrained Natural-Language Interface for Variational Multi-Physics Finite Element Simulations in FEniCS
